name: Climb to Olías with panoramic views of Montes de Málaga – circular from Torre del Mar
name_original: Subida a Olías con vistas panorámicas a Montes de Málaga – circular desde Torre del Mar
region_intro: Road cycling around Rincón De La Victoria offers a diverse landscape that blends coastal beauty with mountainous terrain. The region features a well-maintained coastal path providing mostly flat sections with views of the Mediterranean Sea, including unique tunnels carved from rock. Inland, routes ascend into the Axarquía region, characterized by rolling hills and steady climbs, offering panoramic views. This area provides varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels, combining sea views with mountain scenery. Best road cycling routes around Rincón De La Victoria The most popular road cycling route is From Rincon de la Victoria to Almachar – circular, a 32.3 miles (51.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through inland hills. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is  Casa Fuerte Bezmiliana – Caution: Slippery Road loop from Rincón de la Victoria, a moderate 27.0 miles (43.5 km) path. This route includes varied terrain with coastal sections and inland ascents. Local road cyclists also love the Torre del Mar Lighthouse – Torre del Mar loop from Conjunta Rubimar, a 25.3 miles (40.7 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Road cycling around Rincón De La Victoria is defined by coastal paths, rolling hills, and scenic viewpoints overlooking the Mediterranean. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal spins to challenging inland climbs. The routes in Rincón De La Victoria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Rincón De La Victoria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Rincón De La Victoria?
A: Rincón De La Victoria offers a diverse landscape for road cycling. You'll find well-maintained coastal paths with mostly flat sections and stunning Mediterranean views, including unique tunnels carved from rock. Inland, routes ascend into the Axarquía region, characterized by rolling hills and steady climbs, providing panoramic views and more challenging terrain.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: Yes, there are several routes suitable for beginners. The coastal path extending from Málaga to Rincón de la Victoria offers mostly flat terrain, ideal for a relaxed spin. An example of an easier route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38239205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Mar Lighthouse – Torre del Mar loop from Conjunta Rubimar&lt;/a&gt;, which is 40.7 km long and has minimal elevation gain.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For more challenging rides, head inland towards the Axarquía region. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930412200/from-rincon-de-la-victoria-to-almachar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Rincon de la Victoria to Almachar – circular&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain, with over 1000 meters of climbing over 51.9 km. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14670246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Olías Village – Bolina Pass loop from Rincón de la Victoria&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1100 meters of ascent.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see while cycling?
A: The region is rich in scenic spots. Along the coast, you'll encounter the Rincón de la Victoria Cliffs with natural lookouts and tunnels. Inland, routes often provide panoramic views, especially from elevated points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/684887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolina Pass&lt;/a&gt; or the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7532344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;End of the Pass to Olias&lt;/a&gt;, offering vistas over the Mediterranean Sea and traditional white villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6665180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Mountains Between Fuente Reina and Olías&lt;/a&gt; is another notable viewpoint.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: Rincón De La Victoria enjoys over 300 days of sunshine annually, making it an ideal cycling destination year-round. You can enjoy the sea breeze in summer or explore in milder winter temperatures. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for longer rides.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: Yes, the coastal promenade and dedicated bike paths are excellent for family-friendly cycling. These routes are generally flat and separate from vehicle traffic, providing a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages. The section between La Cala del Moral and Rincón de la Victoria, including the unique tunnels, is particularly suitable.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Rincón De La Victoria?
A: Many road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930412200/from-rincon-de-la-victoria-to-almachar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Rincon de la Victoria to Almachar – circular&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34404775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;⭐ Casa Fuerte Bezmiliana – Caution: Slippery Road loop from Rincón de la Victoria&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the parking options for road cyclists in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: Parking is generally available in Rincón De La Victoria, especially near the promenade and main attractions. Many routes start from easily accessible points within the town or nearby villages, where public parking facilities can be found. It's advisable to check specific starting points for the most convenient parking.

Q: Are there places to rent a road bike in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: Yes, Rincón De La Victoria, being a popular cycling destination, has several local shops and rental services where you can rent road bikes. It's recommended to book in advance, especially during peak season, to ensure availability and the right bike size.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: The road cycling routes in Rincón De La Victoria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, combining stunning sea views with challenging inland climbs, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any unique coastal features to explore on a road bike?
A: Absolutely. The coastal path features a unique section with tunnels carved out of rock, originally part of an old train line, between La Cala del Moral and Rincón de la Victoria. This stretch offers a distinctive experience with wonderful views over Malaga Bay and the Rincón de la Victoria Cliffs.

Q: What cultural or historical sites are near the cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can pass by or easily access several interesting sites. Rincón de la Victoria is home to the &lt;a href="https://www.visitanddo.com/en/victory-corner/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Tesoro (Treasure Cave)&lt;/a&gt;, one of Europe's only marine-origin caves. The 18th-century Bezmiliana Fort House is also a notable landmark. Further afield, Málaga city offers sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7262649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Alcazaba of Málaga and the Roman Theatre&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3452793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Málaga Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Rincón De La Victoria?
A: There are approximately 19 road cycling routes available around Rincón De La Victoria, offering a wide range of options from easy coastal rides to difficult inland climbs. These routes cater to various fitness levels and preferences.
tips: Views of a small town 20 km from the capital. Although it is difficult to remember, because coming to Olías is like taking a trip back in time, it is a neighborhood belonging to the municipality of Malaga.

A small hermitage located next to the road dedicated to the Virgen del Carmen. Rebuilt in 1996, after being left in ruins during the civil war. Dated approximately in 1513. It has the images of San Lucas Evangelista and San Antonio, donated by the brothers Estébanez and Juan Antonio Zambrana Andrades.
Great ribbon of tarmac leaving the busy coast working its way up to the very quiet and peaceful town of Olias
The Olías road takes us directly to this elevated town, which is all joy and we can enjoy several viewpoints towards the coast with beautiful views.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of Carmen
